Green Party values have answers where others have offered platitudes and promised change which never arrived.

I will listen to the members of this community and be a force for action, representing those who need their voices to be heard and acting in the best interests of the community as a whole.

I believe in making hope normal again. Hope for a safe, affordable place to live, the ability to provide for our families and the hope of a better future for those who come after us. This is what I stand for.

I have lived in Southfield Ward for more than 40 years. My children (now grown up) were both educated for 8 years at Southfield School, where I was active in the Parent Staff Association and as a parent governor.

I first joined the Ecology Party (as the Green Party was then known) in 1978. Although I’m pleased that many of its environmental policies have been adopted by other parties, only the Green Party will take the urgent action needed to prevent catastrophic climate change and to prepare for its consequences, including the extreme weather which we are already experiencing, food shortages, air pollution and the destruction of our environment and wildlife. At local level, we can conserve energy (such as through insulating homes), reduce fossil fuel use (by improving public transport and encouraging safe walking and cycling) and protect our natural resources.

I spent many years advising companies on employee share ownership. More recently, I have carried out postgraduate research into Climate Change Economics. I am a Director and Vice Chair of a Community Energy Society.

A vote for the Green Party is not wasted. It sends a strong message that we want to keep a world fit for our grandchildren to live in.

Matthew Mellor is originally from Sheffield but has lived in Northfields for over a decade. A resident of Walpole, he lives with his wife and 2 children who attend local schools. Matthew spent 12 years working in local government and a decade working as a contract manager in the NHS. "I'm passionate about social justice and ensuring we have people with liberal values representing our community on Ealing Council. I'm focused on improving our local services and ensuring no vulnerable person in our community gets left behind or forgotten, which is why protecting our local education, health and social care, and communities services is so important". The biggest thing he would change about the Council is it’s bad record of listening to local people. At a ward level, the council must engage better with residents and I will tackle the visible failings of the council including litter, overgrown vegetation, blocked gullies and potholes.

William Francis works at a HGV driving school and does freelance media analysis work as well. He is also keen on championing local concerns, saying: “I aim to be the best local champion I can be for Walpole and ensure that residents have a strong voice in the council. I am determined to reduce the crime and anti-social behaviour in hot spot areas in the ward, to ensure streets are clean, and to alleviate the housing crisis.”

William is Chair of North West London Young Liberals and Vice Chair of London Young Liberals.

Ksenia Maximova, a former international model and founder of Russian Democratic Society, said: “I have lived in Ealing since 1999 and attended Notting Hill and Ealing High School. I am engaged with local issues: campaigning for cleaner streets, fixing infrastructure problems and advocating for better public spaces.”

Long time volunteer with Lager Can helping to keep our local streets and parks clean with her family.

Managed a crowd funded project to do ground water surveys, buy diggers and created a pond as well as funding bins to keep the area clean.

Founder of the Russian Democratic Society, a London-based organisation that opposes Putin and speaks out against the invasion of Ukraine.

Barry has lived in Ealing since 2014, and has a son at a local primary school.  He is passionate about the environment, and he and his family love watching the wildlife and flora in the local parks and exploring natural areas in the borough.  He is a keen cyclist, and having safe cycling routes for all, especially young children, is important for encouraging more active and integrated travel.  Other areas of particular importance are access to green spaces, trees, lowering pollution levels, and social and economic fairness.

I have lived in the Walpole area of Ealing for over thirty years. I moved here whilst I was working at the BBC’s Community Programme Unit producing and directing campaigning programmes on many social issues such as the immigration system, unemployment and bias in the media. I continued the spirit of this work into my subsequent time at Channel 4, commissioning many programmes concerned with human rights and global development. I spent the last phase of my career as Managing Director of an independent production company before retiring a few years ago. I now volunteer as a teacher of English as a foreign language at a community school in Ealing. I have been drawn to the Green Party’s positive campaigning on environmental, racial and social justice for some time and joined the Party in 2025.

I am particularly interested in supporting policies which: address Ealing’s distressing lack of social housing with developments that meet the needs of local communities; support Ealing’s status as a Borough of Sanctuary; resist future threats to our environment and health such as airport expansion; enhance and expand bio-diversity programmes for the benefit of everyone who lives in the borough.

I am honoured to be standing as a Green Party candidate for Walpole.

Since moving to Ealing, I’ve grown to love life here – from evening walks in Walpole or Lammas Park to catching up with friends in our local cafes.

Having lived in Germany from the age of 10, I moved back to the UK to study marketing with economics at university. Over the years, I undertook a broad range of jobs – working in shops, pubs, kitchens, fundraising, volunteering and currently digital marketing. I later moved here with my partner, who works in youth justice.

As a member of Ealing Green Party I’ve had the pleasure of regularly speaking to local residents and campaigners, hearing about the issues facing our community – from fly-tipping to protecting Warren Farm. If elected, my first priority will always be to listen and provide a powerful voice to all of Walpole’s residents.

I believe my love for the area, understanding of local issues, and broad experience has given me a holistic view that would help me serve the community.

Carl is an energetic, passionate socialist candidate who has lived and worked across West London, witnessing firsthand the corporate decay and bureaucratic mismanagement affecting local communities. A former organiser in a university rent strike campaign and now working in Housing, he has long been committed to standing up for residents. Carl is running in Walpole to protect the area from instability and to safeguard and strengthen the community’s greatest assets for the future.

Richard has lived in Ealing permanently for over ten years but known the area for much longer. Richard is an educator and manager and has worked for theatre companies across the country. He was an active campaigner for Sister Supporter which stopped the harassment of women on Mattock Lane. As a democratic socialist, Richard was previously in the Labour Party but left five years ago in complete despair at the machinations of the local Labour Party in Ealing, and fearing they had abandoned all their principles. He believes that any political party should first serve the interests of the residents, the people it represents, not the interest of those who lead or fund it. As such, he helped form Ealing Community Independents with people from the community properly representing the interests of the community. He supports divestment of the pension scheme away from weapon systems and arms manufacturers and into green renewable industries and technologies.

Darius has been a resident of Ealing since birth and is  proud to call the borough his home. As a democratic socialist he joined Ealing Community Independents to try to help improve the lives of residents in the area he loves, after being disappointed by the continued failure of the Ealing Labour council, and has campaigned for the divestment of local council tax funding weapons companies. Darius has worked as an articulated lorry driver all across London and has a strong interest in the maintenance of our roads and transport network. He is also very passionate about the preservation of our local parks and wildlife.
